perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party hereby acknowledges and agrees that all of the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is the exclusive proprietary property of the Disclosing Party, is being disclosed solely for the purpose of enabling the parties to conduct the discussions relating to the Transaction is to be used by the Receiving Party only in such limited manner as is permitted by the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party hereby acknowledges and agrees that all of the Secret Data of the Disclosing Party is the exclusive proprietary property of the Disclosing Party, is being disclosed solely for the purpose of enabling the parties to conduct the discussions relating to the Transaction is to be used by the Receiving Party only in such limited manner as is permitted by the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Secret Data' in Section 2 creates uncertainty about what data is considered proprietary and protected. This directly contradicts the definition provided in Section 1 under "Confidential Information," blurring the scope of protection. The contradiction lies in whether the obligations regarding confidential information are applicable to this new term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION 1. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Except as set forth below, "Confidential Information" shall mean and include any financial, operational, technical and other information relating to the present and future businesses and affairs of the party disclosing the information (the "Disclosing Party"), which information is provided to the other party (the "Receiving Party") in connection with the business relationship provided in written, oral, graphic, pictorial or recorded form or stored on computer discs, hard drives, magnetic tape or digital or any other electronic medium (it being understood that oral communications will be confirmed in writing within three (3) working days). It is further understood that the term "Confidential Information" does not mean and include information which: (a) is or subsequently becomes publicly available without the Receiving Party's breach of any obligation owed to the Disclosing Party; (b) prior to disclosure hereunder is within the possession of the Receiving Party, and was obtained by the Receiving Party from a source not under obligation not to disclose such information or any of its Representatives as defined below. (c) is lawfully received by the Receiving Party from a third party (other than the Disclosing Party) having rights to disseminate without restriction such information and such information is received by the Receiving Party from such third party without notice to the Receiving Party of any restriction against its further disclosure; (d) is disclosed with the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party; or
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Secret Data' directly contradicts the definition provided in Section 1 under "Confidential Information." The definition in Section 1 explicitly defines 'Confidential Information', and replacing that term with 'Secret Data' creates uncertainty about whether the obligations apply to this new term, thus causing a contradiction. The obligations related to 'Confidential Information' may not apply to 'Secret Data', creating a direct conflict in enforcement.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information shall (a) be kept confidential by the Receiving Party and not disclosed to any third party (except as provided in this Section 3) and (b) not be used by the Receiving Party for any commercial or competitive purpose whatsoever and may only be used in connection with the discussions relating to the Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Protected Data shall (a) be kept confidential by the Receiving Party and not disclosed to any third party (except as provided in this Section 3) and (b) not be used by the Receiving Party for any commercial or competitive purpose whatsoever and may only be used in connection with the discussions relating to the Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: Substituting 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data' in Section 3 introduces a new term without defining it. This creates uncertainty about whether 'Protected Data' encompasses the same scope as 'Confidential Information' as defined in Section 1. The obligations outlined for 'Confidential Information' may not clearly apply to 'Protected Data,' potentially weakening the protection of certain information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION 1. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Except as set forth below, "Confidential Information" shall mean and include any financial, operational, technical and other information relating to the present and future businesses and affairs of the party disclosing the information (the "Disclosing Party"), which information is provided to the other party (the "Receiving Party") in connection with the business relationship provided in written, oral, graphic, pictorial or recorded form or stored on computer discs, hard drives, magnetic tape or digital or any other electronic medium (it being understood that oral communications will be confirmed in writing within three (3) working days). It is further understood that the term "Confidential Information" does not mean and include information which: (a) is or subsequently becomes publicly available without the Receiving Party's breach of any obligation owed to the Disclosing Party; (b) prior to disclosure hereunder is within the possession of the Receiving Party, and was obtained by the Receiving Party from a source not under obligation not to disclose such information or any of its Representatives as defined below. (c) is lawfully received by the Receiving Party from a third party (other than the Disclosing Party) having rights to disseminate without restriction such information and such information is received by the Receiving Party from such third party without notice to the Receiving Party of any restriction against its further disclosure; (d) is disclosed with the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party; or
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data'. Section 1 defines 'Confidential Information' explicitly. The changed text in Section 3 uses 'Protected Data' without defining it. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether 'Protected Data' is subject to the same obligations and protections as 'Confidential Information' as defined in Section 1. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition: 'Misaligned terminology also leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.'

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party and its Representatives hereby agree to use their best efforts to prevent inadvertent disclosure of Confidential Information to others. The Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information with at least the degree of care that it treats similar materials of its own, or a higher standard of care if reasonable under the circumstances. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all documents which contain Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, and agree that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will not retain any copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party and its Representatives hereby agree to use their best efforts to prevent inadvertent disclosure of Private Data to others. The Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information with at least the degree of care that it treats similar materials of its own, or a higher standard of care if reasonable under the circumstances. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all documents which contain Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, and agree that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will not retain any copies thereof.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 4, changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Data' in the first sentence, while retaining 'Confidential Information' in subsequent sentences, introduces a contradiction. It is unclear whether the 'best efforts to prevent disclosure' applies to all confidential information or only to data categorized as 'private data'. This creates confusion about the standard of care required for different types of data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION 4. CARE AND RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Receiving Party and its Representatives hereby agree to use their best efforts to prevent inadvertent disclosure of Private Data to others. The Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information with at least the degree of care that it treats similar materials of its own, or a higher standard of care if reasonable under the circumstances. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all documents which contain Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, and agree that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will not retain any copies thereof.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Data' in the first sentence of Section 4, while retaining 'Confidential Information' in subsequent sentences within the same section, introduces a contradiction. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the scope of obligations, as it's unclear whether the duty to prevent disclosure applies to all confidential information or only to 'private data,' as described in the Perturbation Explanation. The Contradicted Text is the Changed Text, which further solidifies the confusion since it is in the same section.

perturbation.original_text: Neither the execution of this Agreement nor the furnishing of any Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement shall be construed as granting the Receiving Party or its Representatives, either expressly or by implication, any license or right to use any Confidential Information for its own benefit or the benefit of any other Person, firm or entity, and each party hereto expressly agrees not to so use any such information except as otherwise provided herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither the execution of this Agreement nor the furnishing of any Company Secrets pursuant to this Agreement shall be construed as granting the Receiving Party or its Representatives, either expressly or by implication, any license or right to use any Confidential Information for its own benefit or the benefit of any other Person, firm or entity, and each party hereto expressly agrees not to so use any such information except as otherwise provided herein.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 5, replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Company Secrets' specifically in the context of 'furnishing' information, while keeping the rest of the section referring to 'Confidential Information,' introduces ambiguity. This creates an in-text contradiction and raises questions about whether the prohibition on use applies only to 'Company Secrets' furnished under the agreement or to all 'Confidential Information' as defined earlier.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION 5. NO LICENSES. Neither the execution of this Agreement nor the furnishing of any Company Secrets pursuant to this Agreement shall be construed as granting the Receiving Party or its Representatives, either expressly or by implication, any license or right to use any Confidential Information for its own benefit or the benefit of any other Person, firm or entity, and each party hereto expressly agrees not to so use any such information except as otherwise provided herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information' consistently. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Company Secrets' in the context of furnishing information in Section 5, while the rest of the section still refers to 'Confidential Information'. This creates an in-text contradiction, as it becomes unclear whether the restriction on use applies only to 'Company Secrets' or to all 'Confidential Information' as defined elsewhere, leading to misaligned terminology and ambiguity, creating an in-text contradiction as defined in the prompt.
